title: [Quadlet][Rootless] Systemd service prematurely exits
body:
### Issue Description

In *rootless* mode, the systemd service ends up terminating+cleaning up almost immediately after daemonizing the podman process. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
0. Tested using the latest release (4.4.4)
1. Copy [1] to `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/containers/systemd/redis.container`
2. `systemctl --user daemon-reload`
3. `systemctl --user start redis.service`

[1]
```
[Unit]
Description=Redis container

[Container]
Image=docker.io/redis
PublishPort=6379:6379

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```


### Describe the results you received

Service in an inactive state soon after running `start`, with the ExecStopPost cleanup commands executed.
```
○ redis.service - Redis container
     Loaded: loaded (/home/user/.config/containers/systemd/redis.container; generated)
     Active: inactive (dead) since Fri 2023-04-07 12:18:56 PDT; 4s ago
   Duration: 4ms
    Process: 904468 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name=systemd-redis --cidfile=/run/user/1000/redis.cid --replace --rm --log-driver passthrough --cgroups=split --sdnotify=conmon -d --publish 6379:6379 docker.io/redis (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
    Process: 904525 ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm -f -i --cidfile=/run/user/1000/redis.cid (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
    Process: 904579 ExecStopPost=rm -f /run/user/1000/redis.cid (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
   Main PID: 904468 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
        CPU: 68ms

Apr 07 12:18:56 host systemd[1143]: Starting Redis container...
Apr 07 12:18:56 host systemd[1143]: Started Redis container.
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expect the service in an active running state. My understanding is that the service files generated here are of Type=Notify, and the flags sent to podman intend for us to use the container monitor (conmon) to send the correct signals to systemd. This doesn't appear to be happening, and the service appears to just terminate right after running the podman-run command.

Looking at the timestamps, there would should be a much bigger gap since systemd would wait for the start timeout to exceed and then kill the container.  The timestamps indicate though that the container dies right after being started.

Can you try running another container with Quadlet just doing a simple `sleep infinity`?

It looks like something's attempting to stop the service right after it has been started.  The timestamps in between starting and stopping the container is exactly 10 seconds which is the default stop timeout of Podman; after 10 seconds Podman will send SIGKILL if SIGTERM didn't work - a classic when using `sleep`. `redis`, on the other hand, seems to deal with SIGTERM immediately and hence stops.


I believe the fork point is [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/3657d1f548483908903f9e5e7ba782c00c82ec75/pkg/domain/infra/abi/system.go#L92).
* A conditional in this function is the only place where I see a reference to creating a `podman-%d.scope`.
* Stepping in, I see some calls to [`StartTransientUnitContext`](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/3657d1f548483908903f9e5e7ba782c00c82ec75/utils/utils_supported.go#L45), which I see some dbus messages for only on the host machine:
```
May 15 17:22:53 host systemd[1173]: Got message type=method_call sender=:1.228 destination=org.freedesktop.systemd1 path=/org/freedesktop/systemd1 interface=org.freedesktop.systemd1.Manager member=StartTransientUnit  cookie=2 reply_cookie=0 signature=ssa(sv)a(sa(sv)) error-name=n/a error-message=n/a
```

If I attempt to run [`UserOwnsCurrentSystemdCgroup`](https://github.com/containers/common/blob/main/pkg/cgroups/cgroups_supported.go#L42) in a dummy systemd container, I always get `false` on the host, whereas I get `true` in the VM.

Some thoughts on reading some more:

* `net_cls` is a cgroup v1 controller [1]
* By default, Arch tells systemd to start with the cgroup v2 unified hierarchy [2]

With Mullvad attempting to introduce `net_cls`, it is effectively tainting unified hierarchy - so users should really be running in hybrid mode, or revert to legacy [3]. But to my knowledge, hybrid mode isn't given full support in Podman (correct me if I'm mistaken), so I would have to go back to legacy mode, which is a real shame. So while I could attempt to send up a quick patch to update `UserOwnsCurrentSystemdCgroup`, it would be better for Mullvad to have cgroupv2 support.
